title: Parent Rights and Responsibilities
description: Parents in Conroe ISD have both rights and responsibilities when it comes to their child's education. The district recognizes that families play an important role in supporting student success and outlines what parents can expect from the school system, as well as what the district expects from families in return. Understanding these rights and responsibilities helps parents stay informed and engaged in their child's learning experience.

timeline_local.summary: This update creates a new "Parental Engagement" section in Policy FA (Local) for Conroe ISD, adding three new provisions: an online portal for parents to submit comments to administrators and the Board, a requirement that enrolled parents be allowed to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of board meetings, and a requirement that board meetings be held outside of regular business hours.
changes_added: Parents of students enrolled in the District may submit comments to campus or District administrators and the Board of Trustees through an online portal made available on the District's website.

Parents of students currently enrolled in the District shall be allowed to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of any public board meeting [See BED.]. The District shall make provision to ensure that parents are identified.
Board meetings shall be held outside of regular business hours. [See BE.]
timeline_local.notes: All text on the redline page (page 2) appears in red underline, indicating this is entirely new language being added to what was previously a blank or minimal local policy. The section heading "Parental Engagement" is also red underlined, indicating it is new. No red strikethrough (removed) text was present.

timeline_local.summary: This update establishes a new "Parental Engagement" section in Policy FA (Local) for Conroe ISD, adding three new provisions: an online portal for parents to submit comments to administrators and the Board, a priority speaking right for enrolled students' parents during public board meetings, and a requirement that board meetings be held outside of regular business hours.
changes_added: Parents of students enrolled in the District may submit comments to campus or District administrators and the Board of Trustees through an online portal made available on the District's website.

Parents of students currently enrolled in the District shall be allowed to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of any public board meeting [See BED.]. The District shall make provision to ensure that parents are identified.
Board meetings shall be held outside of regular business hours. [See BE.]
timeline_local.notes: All text on the redline page (page 2) appears in red underlined format, indicating this is entirely new language being added to a previously blank or nonexistent local policy. The section heading "Parental Engagement" is also red and underlined, confirming it is new. No red strikethrough (removed) text was present.

description_local: This policy establishes the rights and responsibilities of parents in their children's education within Conroe ISD, outlining how families can be involved in and informed about school decisions and activities. It applies to all parents and guardians of students enrolled in the district. This policy matters to families because it defines the ways the district is committed to keeping parents informed and engaged, and clarifies what parents can expect — and what is expected of them — as partners in their children's education.
